analysis_result: CYCLIN_D1_UP.V1_UP in MPNST-G1 (vs MPNST-G2) tumors: NES = -1.26, FDR q-value = 0.262 (nominal p = 0.076, ES = -0.314). The negative NES indicates the gene set is NOT enriched in MPNST-G1 (it is enriched in MPNST-G2 under this phenotype convention, G1 = positive phenotype). Result is not statistically significant at FDR<0.25.
denominator_used: N = 12 MPNST tumors with available RNA-seq data (6 MPNST-G1: M803, M1014, M2252, M2346, M2372, M3048; 6 MPNST-G2: M1677, M2325, M2377, M2491, M4719, M9413). One MPNST-G2 sample with RNA label 'RT1933' (M1933) was excluded because no matching column existed in GSE207400_Matrix.txt.gz. Gene-set: CYCLIN_D1_UP.V1_UP (n=188 genes) from MSigDB C6 (Oncogenic Signatures).
assumptions_made: Used signal-to-noise statistic (m1-m2)/(sd1+sd2) as the ranking metric with MPNST-G1 as the positive phenotype; applied GSEA minimum variance safeguard (sd floored at 0.2*|mean|); ran gseapy.prerank with 1000 permutations and seed=42 against the full MSigDB_Oncogenic_Signatures C6 collection (189 sets) so that FDR q-values are calibrated across the collection; gene symbols already present as row IDs in GSE207400_Matrix.txt.gz were used directly after averaging any duplicate symbols; the RNA-seq matrix is already log-normalized (as stated in data context); Supplementary Figure 6a likely used classical Broad GSEA rather than gseapy prerank, so numerical values may differ slightly from the paper but sign, magnitude, and significance level should be reproducible.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
- Clinical data: 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx (108 rows, 15 columns). Relevant columns: Tumor_ID, Tumor Type, Methylation_Group, RNA label.
- Expression matrix: GSE207400_Matrix.txt.gz (14995 genes x 48 samples). Already log-normalized (min=-4.32, max=15.40, mean=4.03). Row IDs are HGNC gene symbols.

B. COHORT DEFINITION
- Filter 1: Tumor Type = MPNST and Methylation_Group in {MPNST_G1, MPNST_G2} -> 16 tumors
- Filter 2: Has non-null RNA label -> 13 tumors (6 G1, 7 G2)
- Filter 3: RNA label maps to a column in the expression matrix (tried Tumor_ID, 'M'+RNA label, and RNA label itself) -> 12 tumors (6 G1, 6 G2). RT1933/M1933 dropped (not in matrix).
- Final analytic cohort: 12 MPNST tumors
  * MPNST-G1 (phenotype, n=6): M803, M1014, M2252, M2346, M2372, M3048
  * MPNST-G2 (control, n=6): M1677, M2325, M2377, M2491, M4719, M9413

C. VARIABLE CODING
- Phenotype: Methylation_Group. G1 = positive class, G2 = negative class (as instructed in the question).
- Gene-set: 'CYCLIN D1 UP.V1 UP' from MSigDB_Oncogenic_Signatures via gseapy.get_library (188 genes in set).
- Ranking metric: signal-to-noise ratio s2n = (mean_G1 - mean_G2) / (sd_G1 + sd_G2), with sd_i floored at 0.2*|mean_i| (Broad GSEA default fuzz factor). This is the classical GSEA phenotype-based ranking statistic when permutation is done on gene sets (prerank).

D. COMPUTATION
- Averaged any duplicate gene symbols in the matrix (14992 unique genes after dedup).
- Computed s2n per gene across the 12 samples.
- Ranked genes descending -> .rnk file.
- Ran gseapy.prerank on the full MSigDB_Oncogenic_Signatures library (189 sets) with permutation_num=1000, seed=42, min_size=10, max_size=5000.
- Extracted the row corresponding to CYCLIN_D1_UP.V1_UP.

E. RESULT (CYCLIN_D1_UP.V1_UP, MPNST-G1 vs MPNST-G2, G1 = phenotype)
- ES = -0.3139
- NES = -1.2597 (~ -1.26)
- Nominal p-value = 0.0762
- FDR q-value = 0.2623 (~ 0.26)
- Interpretation: CYCLIN_D1_UP.V1_UP is enriched in MPNST-G2 rather than MPNST-G1 in this recomputation, but the enrichment is not significant at the conventional FDR<0.25 threshold (borderline).
- For context, the other CYCLIN-D1 oncogenic sets in the same run: CYCLIN_D1_UP.V1_DN NES=+1.01/FDR=0.55; CYCLIN_D1_KE.V1_UP NES=-0.86/FDR=0.89; CYCLIN_D1_KE.V1_DN NES=-0.92/FDR=0.81.

F. CAVEATS
- Small sample size (n=6 per group) and permutation-based nulls yield coarse p-value/FDR grids.
- One MPNST-G2 sample could not be mapped to the expression matrix, limiting G2 to 6 rather than 7 tumors.
- The published Supplementary Figure 6a was likely generated with Broad GSEA (Java desktop) rather than gseapy prerank; the ranking metric, permutation scheme (phenotype vs gene-set), random seed, and background collection may all differ, so exact NES/FDR may not match the figure even when the biological conclusion is the same.
- FDR q-values depend on which collection is used as the background; running with only the single CYCLIN_D1_UP.V1_UP set gives FDR = 0.076 (equal to nominal p-val); running against the full 189-set C6 collection gives FDR = 0.262 (the value reported above, which is the standard Broad-style usage).
